NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED A BRIEF TORNADO DAMAGED THREE FARMS. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MPH. ITS PATH LENGTH WAS 0.7 MILES. OTHER DAMAGE IN THE AREA WAS DUE TO DOWNBURSTS.
WEATHER SPOTTERS REPORTED BRIEF TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR 5 MINUTES. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN AN OPEN FIELD AND TIPPED OVER AN IRRIGATION SYSTEM. IT THEN TORE TIN OFF SOME OUTBUILDINGS AND BROKE SEVERAL DOZEN TREES. THE TORNADO WAS SEEN ON SEVERAL VIDEOS. PATH LENGTH WAS 5.7 MILES. IT WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 70 MPH.
PICTURE VIA TWITTER.

UPDATES PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM STOCKHOLM. SPOTTER REPORTED TORNADO ON THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER SOUTH OF STOCKHOLM. THE TORNADO WAS ALSO ON VIDEO. ABOUT ONE DOZEN TREES WERE BROKEN AS IT MOVED UP THE BLUFFS EAST OF STOCKHOLM. ITS PATH LENGTH WAS 1.2 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 70 MPH. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
SPOTTER REPORTED TORNADO MOVED ACROSS RIVER SOUTH OF STOCKHOLM. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
DOZENS OF TREES WERE BROKEN, INCLUDING AT A HOME EAST OF KNAPP. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 0.7 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH.

UPDATES PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 4 E CEDAR FALLS. VIDEO ON TWITTER. NWS SURVEY CONFIRMED TORNADIC DAMAGE JUST NORTH OF I-94 NEAR RUSK, WI. THE DAMAGE CONTINUED TO THE NORTHEAST THROUGH WHITETAIL GOLF COURSE. AN IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS TIPPED OVER, AN OUTBUILDING WAS TORN APART, A TRAILER WAS TIPPED OVER, AND HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH, MAKING IT A LOW END EF1 TORNADO, THOUGH AT TIMES THE WINDS WERE NOT AS STRONG AS 90 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 9.3 MILES.
VIDEO ON TWITTER. LOCATION ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

A STORM SURVEY DETERMINED A TORNADO BROKE SEVERAL DOZEN TREES AND RIPPED PART OF A TIN ROOF OFF A SHED. ITS PATH LENGTH WAS 5.6 MILES AND IT TRAVELED INTO SOUTHWEST CARVER COUNTY, DISSIPATING ABOUT ONE MILE NORTHEAST OF ASSUMPTION LAKE. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 75 MPH.

LAST NIGHTS STORMS CAUSED EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE, WITH SEVERAL TREES (AT LEAST) SNAPPED, AND THE LARGEST TREE ON CAMPUS UPROOTED--A 100+ YEAR-OLD HACKBERRY WITH A 4-FOOT DIAMETER TRUNK. LOTS OF LIMB DAMAGE TOO. THE FALCON HEIGHTS HENNEPIN WEST MESONET SITE IS ABOUT .25 MILES FROM THE DAMAGE AND HAD A 59 MPH GUST.
MESSAGED VIA FACEBOOK MESSENGER.

THIS TORNADO MOVED ACROSS THE SOUTHEAST SECTION OF MADISON. A HOUSE HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED, A CAMPER WAS ROLLED, AND THREE LIGHT POLES IN THE BASEBALL FIELD WERE BENT OVER, WITH 1 HAVING THE CEMENT SUPPORT RIPPED OUT OF THE GROUND. THERE WAS ALSO EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE AS WELL. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 0.9 MILES. IT WAS RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH.
MESONET STATION MN033 HANLEY FALLS MN-23 MILE POST 94.

THIS TORNADO CAUSED MAJOR DAMAGE TO A HOUSE, WHICH HAD ITS ROOF COMPLETELY REMOVED AND SEVERAL WALLS THEN COLLAPSED. THE GARAGE WAS DESTROYED. MANY TREES WERE BROKEN OR SNAPPED. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 1.1 MILES. IT WAS RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105 MPH.

LARGE PORTION OF TURKEY BARN SHEET METAL ROOF RIPPED OFF AND SCATTERED AROUND, UP TO 100 YARDS TO THE NE OF BARN. FLYING DEBRIS ALSO APPEARED TO PUNCTURE HOLE IN SIDE OF HOUSE AS WELL AS BLOWING OUT BACK SIDE OF GARAGE. PROPANE TANK PUNCTURED BY SHEET METAL ROOFING.

A FAST MOVING TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE SOUTH SIDE OF DARLING LAKE IN ALEXANDRIA, WHERE IT CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO MANY HOUSES. IT TRACKED ACROSS THE LAKE BEFORE CAUSING MORE TREE AND HOME DAMAGE JUST EAST OF COUNTY ROADS 11 AND 34. IT THEN MOVED ACROSS A SMALL PART OF LAKE CARLOS BEFORE DISSIPATING ON THE WEST SIDE OF LAKE CARLOS. ITS PATH LENGTH WAS 2.5 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS RATED A STRONG EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 110 MPH.

CORRECTS TIME OF PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 2 SSW CLARISSA. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR BROKEN. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS LOST ROOFS. THE DAMAGE PATTERN IN THIS PART OF TODD COUNTY WAS VERY COMPLICATED, BUT GROUND AND AERIAL SURVEYS REVEALED A TORNADO MOVED THROUGH PART OF CLARISSA. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.3 MILES. IT WAS RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH.

IT DISSIPATED JUST NORTHEAST OF CUSHING AFTER BEING ON THE GROUND FOR 2.9 MILES. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF0 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 4 W MILAN FOR SPELLING. TORNADO SPUN UP NEAR THE MINNESOTA RIVER VALLEY ABOUT 4 MILES WEST OF MILAN AND TRACKED NORTH NORTHEAST 50 MILES TO NORTHWEST OF GLENWOOD (45.68, -95.47). THERE WAS A SMALL GAP BETWEEN THIS TORNADO AND THE FORADA TORNADO AND MORE INVESTIGATION IS NEEDED TO SEE IF IT WAS A SINGLE TRACK. FOR NOW, THE ENDING TIME IS 423 PM. NEAR CONTINUOUS TREE DAMAGE WAS FOUND ALONG THE TRACK. SOME FARM OUTBUILDINGS AND ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINES WERE DAMAGED. SEVERAL AGRICULTURE IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE OVERTURNED. MAXIMUM WIND WAS 100 MPH AND MAX WIDTH WAS ABOUT 350 YARDS.

TORNADO RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 95 MPH. PATH LENGTH 15.4 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. THE TORNADO TORE THROUGH THE EASTERN SIDE OF EAGLE BEND. IT KNOCKED DOWN OR SNAPPED MANY TREES, PARTIALLY REMOVED ROOFS FROM SEVERAL HOUSES, PEELED TIN OFF THE ROOF OF MULTIPLE SHEDS AND BUILDINGS, AND TOOK THE TOP OFF THREE MASSIVE AND FILLED GRAIN BINS IN EAGLE BEND. IT ENDED ABOUT 4.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF STAPLES WHERE THE SCATTERED TREE DAMAGE PATTERN COULD NO LONGER BE DETERMINED TO BE OF TORNADIC ORIGIN.
DELAYED REPORT - DAMAGE REPORTED IN EAGLE BEND MN, LIKELY TORNADIC.

TORNADO RATED EF1 WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH. PATH LENGTH 18.2 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 400 YARDS. THE TORNADO TORE THROUGH THE WESTERN SIDE OF PLATO THROUGH THE EASTERN SIDE OF LESTER PRAIRIE. IT KNOCKED DOWN OR SNAPPED MANY TREES, COMPLETELY COLLAPSED SEVERAL FARM OUTBUILDINGS, ROLLED A CAMPER, PEELED TIN OFF THE ROOF OF MULTIPLE SHEDS AND OUTBUILDINGS, AND KNOCKED DOWN MULTIPLE POWER POLES ALONG ITS PATH. IT ENDED ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTHWEST OF MONTROSE WHERE THE CONCENTRATED TREE DAMAGE PATTERN COULD NO LONGER BE DETERMINED. IN ADDITION TO THE TORNADO, DOWNBURSTS DAMAGED SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS AND NUMEROUS TREES ACROSS E MCLEOD, NW CARVER, AND S WRIGHT COUNTY.
